slides.title: Introduction to SVM
slides.content: Support Vector Machines (SVM) are supervised learning models used for classification and regression analysis. They work by finding the hyperplane that best divides a dataset into classes.

slides.title: Hyperplane Concept
slides.content: The hyperplane is a decision boundary that separates different classes in the data. The goal of SVM is to find the hyperplane with the maximum margin between the classes.

slides.title: Kernel Trick
slides.content: SVM can efficiently perform a nonlinear classification using the kernel trick, implicitly mapping their inputs into high-dimensional feature spaces.

slides.title: Types of Kernels
slides.content: Common kernel functions include linear, polynomial, and Gaussian radial basis function (RBF). Each kernel transforms the data in different ways to find the optimal hyperplane.

slides.title: Example Code
slides.content: Here's a basic example of how to implement an SVM in Python using Scikit-Learn.

visual_assets.code:
from sklearn import svm
clf = svm.SVC(kernel='linear')
clf.fit(X, y)

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: SVM is a powerful tool for classification and regression. It is particularly effective in high-dimensional spaces and versatile due to the use of kernel functions.
